findings.message: User-supplied input written to database without transformation. post_item() calls create_item() directly, bypassing the documented normalization requirement (strip + lowercase). The codebase defines create_item_normalized() with explicit guidance that 'Callers that accept user input MUST go through this function', but this endpoint skips that requirement.
findings.suggestion: Change line 45 from 'return create_item(session, payload.name)' to 'return create_item_normalized(session, payload.name)' to enforce consistent data transformation for all user-supplied input.

findings.message: Documentation in create_item() docstring warns against direct use with user input, but the function remains public and is called unsafely from routes/items.py post_item() endpoint. The documented safety mechanism (create_item_normalized) is defined but not enforced at the call site.
findings.suggestion: Either make create_item() private (rename to _create_item) to prevent unsafe usage, or add a runtime guard that detects untransformed input and raises an error.
